raw_consumer_statement:
Re : Complaint American Express Dispute Reference : XXXX. XXXX American Express knowingly ( and as a course of policy ) allows cardholders to be victimized by unscrupulous fraudsters. American Express fails in their responsibility to protect their cardholders. Further American Express directly enables and protects the fraudster merchant. Both are unjustly enriched by this behavior. 

Background : - Amex client of 25years was defrauded by a XXXX  travel Scam. 
- As part of the scam they lied to victims and buried in fine print the clause no refunds. 
o This clause directly violates governing Federal law - The law allows no exceptions to the consumer protection requirement of a 72 hour right of recension. 
o Sadly American Express DOES allow an exception to the law for the fraudsters. 
- If it is a RIGHT, how is it American Express so flippantly dismisses it? 
- American Express contends : they are not the court so its ok to enforce blatantly abusive and illegal merchant agreements. But where is the line? Can a merchant hide in the small print : We can triple the price without telling you and charge your Amex with no additional notice Would Amex also enforce this clause of a merchant agreement with cardholder? 
Would the rule a dispute IN FAVOR OF THE MERCHANT Amex contends they have no fiduciary responsibility to their cardholders. We disagree. 
Thanks, XXXX XXXX
